content.text:
[Mon 2026-04-20 13:41 UTC] Shape Sort in `repo/` is too hard to use on phones because the drag targets are too small.

Use the git checkout in `repo/` to prepare a teammate-reviewable hotfix for this issue on `review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix` and push it to the local `origin`.

Keep the review branch limited to the Shape Sort hotfix, preserve unrelated local work while preparing it, and restore that local work after the push.

arguments.command: cd /root/.openclaw/workspace/repo && git status && git log --oneline -8 && git branch -a

arguments.command: cd /root/.openclaw/workspace/repo && find . -type f | grep -i shape | grep -v '.git'

content.text:
<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
  <head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>Shape Sort</title>
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="style.css">
  </head>
  <body>
    <main class="shape-sort-board"></main>
    <script src="game.js"></script>
  </body>
</html>

Good — I can see the problem clearly. The tiles are 72×72px with only 6px drag padding, which is too small for touch targets (Apple/Google guidelines recommend at least 44–48px minimum, and the tiles themselves should be larger on mobile). Now let me set up the hotfix branch cleanly.

arguments.command: cd /root/.openclaw/workspace/repo && git stash push -u -m "wip: local work before shape-sort mobile hotfix" && git stash list

content.text:
Saved working directory and index state On main: wip: local work before shape-sort mobile hotfix
stash@{0}: On main: wip: local work before shape-sort mobile hotfix

details.aggregated:
Saved working directory and index state On main: wip: local work before shape-sort mobile hotfix
stash@{0}: On main: wip: local work before shape-sort mobile hotfix

content.text: Now apply the fixes — larger tiles, bigger drag padding, and a `touch-action` CSS rule to prevent scroll-fighting on mobile:
Clean diff — only Shape Sort files touched. Commit and push:

arguments.command:
cd /root/.openclaw/workspace/repo && git add public/games/shape-sort/style.css public/games/shape-sort/game.js && git commit -m "fix(shape-sort): enlarge touch targets and drag zones on mobile

Tiles were 72×72px with 6px drag padding — too small for reliable
finger interaction on phones.

Changes:
- CSS: add @media (pointer: coarse) block that bumps tiles to 96×96px
  (border-radius scaled proportionally) and sets touch-action: none to
  prevent scroll interference during drag gestures.
- JS: detect coarse pointer at runtime and update shapeSortConfig so
  the grab-hit-test and snap tolerance match the larger visual size
  (dragPaddingPx 6→16, snapTolerancePx 16→24).

Desktop layout and behaviour are unchanged."

content.text:
Everything's back exactly as it was. Here's a summary of what was done:

---

**What changed (Shape Sort only)**

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `style.css` | Added `@media (pointer: coarse)` block — tiles grow from 72×72 → **96×96px**, `touch-action: none` prevents scroll fighting during drags |
| `game.js` | Runtime `matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)')` check updates `shapeSortConfig` — `dragPaddingPx` 6 → **16**, `snapTolerancePx` 16 → **24** |

Desktop is completely unaffected — both changes are gated on coarse-pointer detection.

**Branch hygiene**
- Stashed the unrelated local work (`README.md`, `REVIEW_WORKFLOW.md` deletion, `notes/`, `.env`, `playtest/`) before branching
- Hotfix branch contains exactly one commit touching exactly two files
- Pushed to `origin/review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix`
- Restored all local work to `main` — `git status` matches the original state
